_comment: Claude UI Workflow explainer — SSL 2026 conference (9 May). 7 scenes, ~166 words. Single-stem chapter render: scenes joined with break tags, one ElevenLabs call. Visual concerns (titles, motion, on-screen text) live in src/ClaudeUiWorkflowExplainer.tsx and treatments/claude-ui-workflow-vo.md — this file is voice-generation only.

scenes.text: You're an Amazon seller. You need a landing page. The AI tools you've tried — they give you something that looks like AI made it.
What if you could have agency-quality design — without the agency? Without three weeks. Without twenty grand.
Drop in a URL. The system extracts the brand DNA. Colours. Fonts. Voice. Hierarchy. All of it — locked as your source of truth.
From there, fifteen design databases — a hundred and sixty-five rules — write the brief themselves. Stitch paints the page. The audit catches every drift before you see it.
Three real brands ran the full pipeline. Re Tech UK — twenty-seven out of thirty. Databrill — twenty-seven. Push-Pull Agency — twenty-eight. All shipped first-pass.
Push-Pull took about fifty minutes of operator time. Most of that — making taste calls in Stitch. The system did the rest.
It's free. It's yours. Pull the repo. Run it on your brand. Get to work.
